title: dryopteris pubescens var. muscosa
intro: Dryopteris pubescens var. muscosa, also known as the hairy wood fern, is a variety of the Dryopteris pubescens plant species. This plant is a type of fern that is native to North America and is characterized by its hairy fronds. The hairy wood fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function.
